channel.title: Research Staff on NTU IoT Sensing Group
channel.description: Recent content in Research Staff on NTU IoT Sensing Group
channel.copyright: ©{year}, NTU IoT Sensing Group. All Rights Reserved

item.title: Duc Van Le
item.description: Duc Van Le Position: Research Fellow Contact: vdle@ntu.edu.sg Bio: Duc Van Le is research fellow at the HP-NTU Digital Manufacturing Corporate Lab, Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore. He obtained his B.Eng. degree in electronics and telecommunications engineering from the Le Quy Don Technical University, Vietnam, and PhD degree in computer engineering from the University of Ulsan, South Korea, in 2011 and 2016, respectively. Before joining NTU, he worked as a research fellow at Department of Computer Science, National University of Singapore (NUS) from 2016 to 2018.

item.title: Zhenyu Yan
item.description: Zhenyu Yan Position: Research Fellow Contact: zhenyu.yan@ntu.edu.sg Bio: Zhenyu Yan works as a research fellow in NTU IoT Sensing Group. Previously, he obtained his Ph.D from Nanyang Technological University and his bachelor’s degrees in both Electronic Engineering and Economics from University of Electronic Science and Technology of China (UESTC). His research mainly lies in human-centric Internet-of-Things, including applications with novel modalities, security and privacy of IoT systems.

item.title: Dongfang Guo
item.description: Dongfang Guo Position: Research Associate Contact: dongfang.guo@ntu.edu.sg Bio: I am currently a Research Associate in Prof. Tan’s research group. I received my B.Eng. degree from University of Electronic Science and Technology of China, and my M.Sc. degree from Nanyang Technological University, Singapore. My research interests lie in the field of Internet of Things and Wireless Sensor Networks. I am now digging into LoRa and exploring its application in indoor object localization.

item.title: Jiale Chen
item.description: Jiale Chen Position: Research Associate Contact: +65 88708195 (Mobile phone) jiale.chen@ntu.edu.sg Bio: I am now a research associate in HP/NTU Lab. I received my Master degree from Hong Kong University of Science and Technology in 2019. I got my bachelor degree from Southern China University of Technology in 2018. Research projects: Acoustic and imagery sensing

item.title: Joy Qiping Yang
item.description: Joy Qiping Yang Position: Project Officer Contact: joy.yang@ntu.edu.sg Bio: Joy Yang Qiping is a project officer in HP/NTU Lab. He received degree in data and computer science from the Sun Yat-sen University in 2019. Research projects: Acoustic and imagery sensing
